world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ritualistic architecture; occult symbols; dark robes on human NPCs; demonic creature presence; blood-stained environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and defend; Cabal members use environmental cover; coordinated enemy behavior in faction strongholds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental corruption; increased supernatural entity density; shifting from human enemies to demonic creatures; escalating visual chaos
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive; greater supernatural creature spawning; environmental hazards activate more frequently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy variety increases; environmental complexity escalates; boss encounters become more elaborate; architectural design reflects deepening into Cabal strongholds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density and difficulty increase; combat encounters become more sophisticated; boss patterns grow complex

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct environmental theming shifts (1920s industrial → ritualistic → futuristic); architectural style changes; visual narrative progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types and tactics reset/shift with new location thematic; new hazard types introduced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Intense demonic imagery; supernatural atmospheric effects; reality distortion visual effects; overwhelming infernal aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-level enemy coordination; reality-warping mechanics; overwhelming enemy numbers in final confrontations

environmental_storytelling.example: Mixing 1920s-1940s architecture with futuristic technology and Weird West elements; visual inconsistency communicates 'this world operates under non-standard rules' and supernatural influence
Cabal temples and altar spaces with specific architectural patterns; blood stains and occult symbols on walls convey historical violence and ongoing dark worship
Lava pits, crushing blocks, spike traps positioned to reveal level design intentionality; suggests hostile environments engineered by Cabal for ritual or defense purposes
Dark robes visually mark human enemies as Cabal cultists; demonic creatures carry distinct silhouettes; visual differentiation communicates organizational hierarchy
Episode transitions shift visual aesthetic (industrial → supernatural → futuristic); environment itself tells story of descending deeper into Tchernobog's domain
Keys and doors use distinct color schemes; visual matching system guides navigation without explicit UI; communicates puzzle solutions through environmental design
Boss-level chambers feature enlarged architectural elements and increased vertical space; visual grandeur communicates escalated threat level

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Move through territory methodically; use environmental cover; suggest organized occupation
Aggressive pursuit; coordinated attack patterns; defensive positioning using environment
Erratic movement patterns; unpredictable attacks; suggest otherworldly nature; higher aggression threshold
Coordinated group movement; individually weak but tactically dangerous in numbers; environmental presence suggests infestation
Complex attack patterns; arena-control mechanics; telegraphed abilities; suggest intelligence and supernatural power
Visual manifestation of demon's will; supernatural presence indicates extreme threat level

notes: Blood communicates world-state primarily through visual faction identity (Cabal dark aesthetic vs. demonic supernatural vs. player's gunslinger identity) and environmental architecture that shifts from industrial human spaces to ritual chambers to hellish domains. Danger is signaled through enemy type diversity, environmental hazard density, and visual intensity escalation. The game's anachronistic design (mixing eras and technologies) functions as a macro-level signal that normal world rules don't apply—supernatural forces have corrupted reality. Episode progression visually narrates descent into deeper Cabal/demonic territory. Nuisance enemies as swarms create tactical complexity signaling; individual enemies communicate faction allegiance through costume. Environmental storytelling relies on architectural distinctiveness, ritualistic markers, and hazard placement to communicate areas of active Cabal/demonic control.
